---
title: "Wiki-Grounded Research Agents"
subtitle: "A shared living memory for scientists and their AI agents"
author:
  - name: "**Chris R. Sweet**"
    affiliation: "Center for Research Computing, University of Notre Dame"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    slide-number: c
    incremental: false
    center: false
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    embed-resources: false
    footer: "la3d.github.io/WGRA"
    title-slide-attributes:
      data-background-color: "#14213d"
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <script src="cytoscape.min.js"></script>
    resources:
      - cytoscape.min.js
      - instance-graph-slice.json
---

> The long-serving lab technician who knows every protocol, remembers every failed method, and never leaves.

## Every LLM session starts from zero.

[The problem]{.kicker}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="24%"}
<div class="session-card"><strong>Session 1</strong><span>context re-pasted</span></div>
:::

::: {.column width="8%"}
<div class="session-break"><span class="symbol">∅</span>nothing<br>carries over</div>
:::

::: {.column width="24%"}
<div class="session-card"><strong>Session 2</strong><span>context re-pasted</span></div>
:::

::: {.column width="8%"}
<div class="session-break"><span class="symbol">∅</span>nothing<br>carries over</div>
:::

::: {.column width="24%"}
<div class="session-card"><strong>Session 3</strong><span>context re-pasted</span></div>
:::

::::

<br>

- ChatGPT and Claude conversations do not remember the last one.
- Retrieval over your group's PDFs rediscovers the same chunks on every query.
- Nothing accumulates between conversations. Nothing compounds between collaborators.

::: {.callout-highlight}
When the student who ran the analysis graduates, so does the knowledge.
:::

## The knowledge graph, live on the slide {.smaller}

[Live KG demo · markov_embeddings_and_rag]{.kicker}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="65%"}
```{=html}
<div id="graph" style="width:100%; height:500px; border: 1px solid #ccc; background: #fff; border-radius: 4px;"></div>
```
:::

::: {.column width="35%"}
`markov_embeddings_and_rag`

30 nodes, 39 edges from a real research wiki.

**Gold-highlighted** edges have the same shape as the web_forager cascade:

- 4-hop `extends` chain
- 2 `criticizes` edges

**Random-Walk-With-Restart** is the central hub at 48 inbound edges. Surfaced by the `hub-notes` SPARQL query, not hand-authored.

No Alt-Tab. Drag nodes to interact.
:::

::::

```{=html}
<script>
const typeColor = {
  'Concept':        '#0d4a8b',
  'Synthesis':      '#7a5310',
  'Entity':         '#1b6e2a',
  'SourceSummary':  '#7a5310',
  'Reference':      '#666',
  'Index':          '#6a1b5b',
  'UntypedNote':    '#aaa'
};

function initGraph() {
  if (!window.cytoscape) { setTimeout(initGraph, 100); return; }
  fetch('instance-graph-slice.json').then(r => r.json()).then(data => {
    const cy = cytoscape({
      container: document.getElementById('graph'),
      elements: [
        ...data.nodes.map(n => ({data: n.data})),
        ...data.edges.map(e => ({data: e.data}))
      ],
      style: [
        { selector: 'node', style: {
          'background-color': ele => typeColor[ele.data('type')] || '#888',
          'label': 'data(label)',
          'color': '#1a1a1a',
          'text-outline-color': '#fff',
          'text-outline-width': 2,
          'font-size': 8,
          'font-family': 'Helvetica Neue, Arial, sans-serif',
          'width': 18, 'height': 18,
          'text-wrap': 'wrap',
          'text-max-width': 90
        }},
        { selector: 'node[type = "Concept"]', style: { 'width': 24, 'height': 24 }},
        { selector: 'edge', style: {
          'width': 1,
          'line-color': '#ccc',
          'target-arrow-color': '#ccc',
          'target-arrow-shape': 'triangle',
          'curve-style': 'bezier',
          'font-size': 7,
          'color': '#888'
        }},
        { selector: 'edge[label = "extends"]',    style: { 'line-color': '#0d4a8b', 'target-arrow-color': '#0d4a8b', 'width': 1.4 }},
        { selector: 'edge[label = "supports"]',   style: { 'line-color': '#1b6e2a', 'target-arrow-color': '#1b6e2a', 'width': 1.4 }},
        { selector: 'edge[label = "criticizes"]', style: { 'line-color': '#8b0d3f', 'target-arrow-color': '#8b0d3f', 'width': 1.6 }},
        { selector: 'edge[casestudy = "yes"]', style: {
          'line-color': '#b8860b',
          'target-arrow-color': '#b8860b',
          'width': 3,
          'z-index': 10
        }},
        { selector: 'node:selected', style: { 'border-width': 3, 'border-color': '#b8860b' }}
      ],
      layout: { name: 'cose', idealEdgeLength: 85, nodeOverlap: 14, animate: false, fit: true }
    });
    if (window.Reveal) {
      Reveal.on('ready', () => { cy.resize(); cy.fit(); });
      Reveal.on('slidechanged', () => { cy.resize(); cy.fit(); });
    }
  });
}
initGraph();
</script>
```

::: {.notes}
Say: "This is a different research wiki, same pattern. The gold-highlighted edges have the exact shape as the web_forager cascade I just described — a four-hop extends chain from a leaf finding back through experimental lineage to a foundational concept, and a criticizes edge from that same leaf finding to a page five hops up its own ancestry."

Then drag a node to demonstrate interactivity. Zoom in on the gold cluster.

Advantage over the PowerPoint version: no Alt-Tab to Safari, no browser-tab risk, no fallback screenshots. If Wi-Fi drops during the talk, this slide still works because everything is local.
:::

## Why Quarto for this kind of talk

[Format]{.kicker}

- Slides authored in Markdown. The source you're viewing is `.qmd`, one heading per slide.
- Cytoscape.js loaded once at page level; any slide can drop a `<div>` and render into it.
- No context switch between the story and the demo. The graph is on the slide.
- Same source can render to PDF/Beamer (`--to beamer`) or PowerPoint (`--to pptx`) if a colleague needs a static handout.
- Speaker notes travel with the source; they don't get lost when you re-export.

::: {.callout-highlight}
The demo isn't a browser window you switch to. It's part of the slide.
:::

::: {.notes}
This slide is intentionally boring in layout, to show the contrast: previous slide had a live interactive graph without any special stagecraft; this one is just a bulleted list. Same Markdown source, same output pipeline.
:::
